steps.title: Target dishonest speech
steps.content: The claim to illustrate is specific: Aristophanes satirizes sophists as teaching people to speak dishonestly. The quotation must therefore connect sophistic lessons with deception or dishonest rhetoric, not merely with sophists in general.

steps.title: Reject weaker quotations
steps.content: Choice A shows Strepsiades urging his son toward sophistry for financial reasons. Choice B shows pleasure in cleverness and disrespect for laws. Choice D shows Socrates asking about memory. None of those directly shows instruction in dishonest speech. The answer is C.
